title: Core AI Fabric Recipe
description: A conversion recipe: everything needed to turn one upstream Hugging Face model into a provenance-verified .aimodel artifact. Recipes are the unit of contribution in coreai-fabric.

id.description: Recipe id. Doubles as the coreai-catalog model id at register time, so it must not collide with an existing catalog id.

revision.description: Upstream HF commit sha the recipe was authored against. Optional: leave absent if not resolved; convert re-resolves and pins the exact revision in the conversion manifest.

license.description: Upstream license identifier as declared by the upstream repo (e.g. the HF license tag).

license_terms.description: License triage — mirrors the coreai-catalog upstream.schema vocabulary so the two repos share one contract. permissive = on the fabric allowlist (commercial_use maps to 'likely'); weak_copyleft/restricted/review_required all map to commercial_use 'check_license' (conservative); unknown = not yet triaged. Triage labels are not legal advice.

pipeline_tag.description: Upstream HF pipeline_tag at scaffold time (informational).

size_bytes.description: Upstream repo storage size in bytes as reported by the HF API at scaffold time (informational).

tool.description: Converter executable resolved on PATH at convert time (verified tools: coreai-fabric-llm-export from this repo's [convert] extra, coreai.llm.export from an apple/coreai-models checkout), OR a per-model script path inside an apple/coreai-models checkout (models/<family>/export.py) — script tools are documented but not driven by fabric; convert prints the verified manual invocation.

min_tool_version.description: Minimum version of the underlying converter library (coreai-torch) known to work for this recipe — every verified tool is a thin driver over coreai-torch. Leave absent if unknown; 0.4.1 was validated on real hardware 2026-07-03.

apple_registry_name.description: For tool `coreai.llm.export` (Apple's production exporter): the Apple model-registry short-name (e.g. `qwen3-0.6b`, from `coreai.model.registry --list-models`). When set, convert drives `coreai.llm.export <short-name>` and lets Apple's TESTED compression preset resolve — this produces the production KV-cache chat asset that passes parity. `precision`/`quantization` are then documentation only (the preset wins). Absent = raw HF-ID export (requires `--experimental`, uses this recipe's precision/quantization, and typically does NOT clear the parity bar).

args.description: Extra converter arguments passed through verbatim as --key value pairs (e.g. platform: macOS). For script tools these document the script's verified flags.

quantization.description: Passed to the converter as --compression (verified flag name; e.g. none, 4bit, or a coreai-opt YAML preset stem).

precision.description: Passed to the converter as --compute-precision. Verified vocabulary: float16, bfloat16, float32.

format_version.description: .aimodel format version the bundle is expected to declare. Leave absent if unknown.

bundle_files.description: Paths (relative to the .aimodel bundle root) that must exist after conversion. Checked by verify Gate A.

checks.description: Structural checks run offline by `coreai-fabric verify` (implementable without the Apple runtime).

metric.description: Parity metric vs the upstream model. `graph_output_cosine`/`per_token_logit_cosine` = raw fidelity of a STATIC-graph export (fabric's coreai-fabric-llm-export). `benchmark_accuracy` = the correct metric for a QUANTIZED production `coreai.llm.export` asset (task accuracy, e.g. tinyMMLU, must stay within tolerance of the upstream) — but Apple's evaluator (`coreai.llm.eval`) ships as a stub as of coreai-models 0.1.0, so this gate honestly reports not_run until it lands. See docs/parity-protocol.md.

tolerance.description: Allowed slack below threshold before hard failure.

greedy_token_exact.description: LLMs only: additionally require greedy-decoded tokens to match the upstream exactly.

hf_target_namespace.description: The publisher's OWN Hugging Face namespace (user or org). Fabric never hosts weights.

repo_name.description: Target HF repo name for the published artifact.

catalog.description: Fields needed to generate the coreai-catalog entries at register time. Optional at draft stage; required before `coreai-fabric register`. The catalog rejects `unknown` for the runtime facts and requires bundle_kind + min_os on every model, so the recipe must declare them here — fabric is the converter and is the authoritative source for these conversion-time facts.

bundle_kind.description: Mirrors the catalog model.schema bundle_kind enum. The catalog test suite requires bundle_kind on every model. Choose the kind that matches the model's task (e.g. an LLM chat model = llm, a document-OCR model = ocr).

min_os.description: Minimum OS the .aimodel runtime requires. For apple-core-ai this is macOS/iOS 27.0, grounded in apple/coreai-models Package.swift platforms: [.macOS("27.0"), .iOS("27.0")]. Defaults to 27.0/27.0 at register time if absent.

runtime_facts.description: Runtime characteristics of the converted bundle. The catalog's audit REJECTS `unknown` for these four, so they must be real booleans. A standard clean coreai-fabric export runs on the stock CoreAIRunner with no custom Metal kernel, no zoo-style source patch, and no separate AOT step (the export IS the compile) — declare true/false honestly for THIS recipe's conversion.

stock_runtime.description: Runs on the stock CoreAIRunner without runtime modification.

patch_required.description: Requires a source patch to the runtime (zoo-style). A clean fabric export is false.

aot_required.description: Requires a separate ahead-of-time compile beyond the export.

architecture.description: Mirrors the catalog model.schema architecture enum.

runner.description: Mirrors the catalog model.schema runner enum. Use unknown until verified on-device.

status.description: Pipeline stage. Advanced only by the corresponding fabric command.

published.description: Written back by `coreai-fabric publish` after a successful upload. Required when status is published or registered.

revision.description: HF commit sha of the published artifact repo.

catalog_pr.description: URL of the open catalog PR written back by `register` after `gh pr create`. Status stays `published` until a human MERGES this PR; `register --mark-merged` then flips status to `registered`. Absent until a PR is opened.
